--EcoPoly: 11806

--fair amount of case-hardened CWD suggesting F.l.bb

column25:
--very sparse mosses
--some really big trees were harvested from this site
--looks like quite a lot of dried-up white berries at this site (too much heat an sun causing failure; too dry?)

--although this site may seem almost identifcal to essf78, the understory composition is quite different; Vacc memb cover is quite high as it the berry productivity; what's the difference?

--moss cover fairly contiguous; not spp id's
--for both Vacc and R. acerifolia, the Rs are a blended rating, lots of plants in the lower 1/2 of the plot seem to have produced very minor berry crops beneath the thicker understory; wereas in the upper 1/2 of the plot, open growing conditions have enabled really good looking crop to predominate

column7: Se - Bg - Oplopanax - Athyrium - Gymnocarpium + Smilacina (stellata)
column8: Hope-Princeton Highway in Manning Park ~1 mile W Skaist R. 300m after crossing of Skagit.  10.1 mile inside Park boundary.
column18: Natural-climax Bg C stand or successional Bg Se towards Bg-C.  Overmature Se-Bg stand on alluvial fan.  Quite a few of the Se drilled were rotten whereas the Bg were sound.  C was restricted to A3 and B layers.  A3 Populus trichocarpa off plot.  Very good form for Bg and, to a lesser extent, Se.  A few of both spp were leaning.  Regen of Bg and C few.  Small coverage of mosses.  Both due to lush C layer.  No regen of Se noted.  Charcoal found at 50 cm in soil pit.  3 cases of blowdown of mature trees near plot.  Planting spp- Bg, C and, with some reservation, Se if it is not susceptible to rot at an early age.  Slashburning should be restricted to light intensity burning because of very shallow organic layers.  No evidence of recent flooding.
column19: Flood plain deposits.  Some charcoal in IICgj.  Sandy material deposited during different floods.  No indication of and recent floods.  Coarse fragments are not present and no root impeding layer.  Gleying of the layer two horizons is due to a high water table during some part of the year.
column25: Moisture nutrient # ~6-E.  Moderate shrub layer over dense herb layer- Gymnocarpium in many cases growing beneath Athyrium.  Two large P. engelmannii- one in plot, are leaning but otherwise in good vigor.

column8: Hope-Princeton Highway in Manning Park ~1 mile W Skaist R. 300m after crossing of Skagit.  10.1 mile inside Park boundary.
column18: Natural-climax Bg C stand or successional Bg Se towards Bg-C.  Overmature Se-Bg stand on alluvial fan.  Quite a few of the Se drilled were rotten whereas the Bg were sound.  C was restricted to A3 and B layers.  A3 Populus trichocarpa off plot.  Very good form for Bg and, to a lesser extent, Se.  A few of both spp were leaning.  Regen of Bg and C few.  Small coverage of mosses.  Both due to lush C layer.  No regen of Se noted.  Charcoal found at 50 cm in soil pit.  3 cases of blowdown of mature trees near plot.  Planting spp- Bg, C and, with some reservation, Se if it is not susceptible to rot at an early age.  Slashburning should be restricted to light intensity burning because of very shallow organic layers.  No evidence of recent flooding.
column19: Flood plain deposits.  Some charcoal in IICgj.  Sandy material deposited during different floods.  No indication of and recent floods.  Coarse fragments are not present and no root impeding layer.  Gleying of the layer two horizons is due to a high water table during some part of the year.

column7: Se - Bl - Vaccinium membranaceum - Paxistima myrsinites
column8: Hope Princeton Highway ~1 mile west Manning Park Coldstream Camp
column18: Climax Se.  Stand dominated by Se with Se in all layers.  The B1 was sparse with the larger trees rotten (except #14 which was at the edge of stand near road) no outward signs of decay apart from branch stubs).  Bl also in all layers of canopy but much less frequent than Se.  Tsuga heterophylla noted off plot about 2m high and very contorted.  Plot included an area in the open and in a slight depression, <1m, where sitka alder was dominant (about 1/5 of plot).  A few blowdown trees near plot.  The second prism sweep was closer to the road and may have included trees now removed for road construction.  Two Populus trichocarpa (too difficult to est. age from rings dbh- 73.0 cm) noted in area in A2 (also- close to plot 3) moderately good vigor.  Se in combination with B1 would be spp for planting, light spot burning could be tolerated.  This plot was at Se Parent Tree #13, for information on P.T.s see I. Davis, Reforestation Division BCFS Vancouver.
column19: Humus form: Orthifibrihumimor.  Morainal blanket.  Bhfj2, Bfj and Bmgj are compacted such that root penetration is low.  Bf1 shows reddening due to Fe accumulation and oxidation, but isn't severe enough to be called  Bf horizon.  Coarse fragments are mostly rounded due to the action of water and glaciation.  Bulk density sample of the Bfj (BD 5) will also apply to the Bhfj2 horizon as they are of similar textures and coarse fragment content.  The pit was dug in plot 2 and will be similar to the soil found in plot 3.
column25: Characterized by dense shrub layer covered by a light tree layer.  Trees are not too large except for the P. engelmanni, and Hw is definitely of poor vigor.  ~4-5/D.

column25: Characterized by dense shrub layer covered by a light tree layer.  Trees are not too large except for the P. engelmanni, and Hw is definitely of poor vigor.  ~4-5/D.

column7: Se - Alnus sitchensis - Equisetum (arvense?) Gymnocarpium dryopteris
column8: further in stand from plot 2, Hope-Princeton Highway Manning Park nr. Coldstream campground
column18: Climax Se.  Two blowdown trees on this plot.  This plot is wetter and richer than adjacent one (035179) and this is characterized by greater significance of Equisetum and Gymnocarpium.  The boundaries of the plot are around and opening with only A3 trees and mostly Alnus sitchensis and Cornus stolonifera.  The open condition of this plot may be caused by the wetter conditions giving rise to a dense C layer crowding out regen and in combination with the blowdown.  Se would do well on this plot and light intensity burning could be tolerated.  Pl (dbh- 51, A2 tree) on slightly drier site near plot- good growth for 50 yrs. beyond 100 yrs very slow growth.  Total age 160 yrs.  No soil description was done for this plot as the adjacent plot (035179) was considered to adequately describe soils here.

column18: Climax Se.  Two blowdown trees on this plot.  This plot is wetter and richer than adjacent one (035179) and this is characterized by greater significance of Equisetum and Gymnocarpium.  The boundaries of the plot are around and opening with only A3 trees and mostly Alnus sitchensis and Cornus stolonifera.  The open condition of this plot may be caused by the wetter conditions giving rise to a dense C layer crowding out regen and in combination with the blowdown.  Se would do well on this plot and light intensity burning could be tolerated.  Pl (dbh- 51, A2 tree) on slightly drier site near plot- good growth for 50 yrs. beyond 100 yrs very slow growth.  Total age 160 yrs.  No soil description was done for this plot as the adjacent plot (035179) was considered to adequately describe soils here.
column19: Soil pit not done.  Humus form: Orthifibrihumimor.
column25: This plot represents an opening adjacent to area with soil pit.  The opening appears to be due to the excess moisture in the soil.  A few windfalls are in plot, but have mainly come from edge of plot.  ~5-6/E.

column7: Sx - Bl - rhodalb - valesit glacial meltwater channel
column8: Pacific Coast Trail, Manning Park
column18: Lush, rich understorey. SNR C-D. Seepage site at toe slopes despite coarse soils. Site conditions more 110, veg a mix of 110 and 1111. Meltwater channel - A1 Sx killed by beetles - still standing but dead, so canopy is more open. At thoe slope, but level in melt water channel.
column19: hard to tell b/c soil so dark - some charcoal layers throughout. Evidence of slope wash.
column25: lots of dead Sx in canopy. Would have been A1's but dead now. Rich lush understory. Latering in A canopy - lots of overlap
